feed.title: Astolfo’s Home
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: Evaluate \(\int_0^1 \frac{\ln x}{x^2-x-1}\,\mathrm{d}x\) by Residue Theorem
entry.content:
Let

\[I=\int_0^1 \frac{\ln x}{x^2-x-1}\,\mathrm{d}x.\]

Factor

\[x^2-x-1=(x-\alpha)(x-\beta),\qquad \alpha=\frac{1+\sqrt5}{2},\;\beta=\frac{1-\sqrt5}{2}=-\frac1\phi,\quad \phi=\frac{1+\sqrt5}{2}.\]

Then

\(\frac{1}{x^2-x-1}=\frac1{\sqrt5}\left(\frac{1}{x-\beta}-\frac{1}{x-\alpha}\right),\) so

\[I=\frac1{\sqrt5}(J_\beta-J_\alpha),\qquad J_c:=\int_0^1 \frac{\ln x}{x-c}\,\mathrm{d}x.\]

Residue part

For

\(F_c(s)=\int_0^1 \frac{x^s}{x-c}\,\mathrm{d}x,\) using a keyhole contour for (\frac{\log z}{z-c}) with branch cut on ((0,\infty)), differentiating at (s=0) gives

\(J_c=\frac{\operatorname{Li}_2(1/c)}{c},\) where (\operatorname{Li}_2) is the dilogarithm.

Hence

\[I=\frac1{\sqrt5}\left(\frac{1}{\beta}\operatorname{Li}_2\left(\frac1\beta\right)-\frac{1}{\alpha}\operatorname{Li}_2\left(\frac1\alpha\right)\right) =\frac1{\sqrt5}\left(-\phi\,\operatorname{Li}_2(-\phi)-\frac1\phi\operatorname{Li}_2\left(\frac1\phi\right)\right).\]

Using standard dilogarithm identities and (\phi^2-\phi-1=0), this simplifies to

\[I=\frac{\pi^2}{5\sqrt5}.\]

Therefore

\[\boxed{\int_0^1 \frac{\ln x}{x^2-x-1}\,\mathrm{d}x=\frac{\pi^2}{5\sqrt5}}.\]

Numerically,

\[\frac{\pi^2}{5\sqrt5}\approx 0.8827642541.\]
entry.summary: A residue theorem evaluation of a logarithmic real integral.
